Vata.general_principles: Vata dosha is governed by air and space elements. Balance through warmth, regularity, grounding foods, and calming practices. Avoid cold, dry, and irregular habits.

favor: Ghee, sesame oil, and warming spices (ginger, cumin, cinnamon)
Warm milk with turmeric or ashwagandha
Whole grains like rice and wheat
avoid: Excess bitter, pungent, and astringent tastes
dietary_guidelines.meal_timing: Eat at fixed times; never skip meals. Largest meal at noon. Light, warm dinner before 7 PM.
dietary_guidelines.taste_preference: Sweet, sour, and salty tastes pacify Vata. Avoid excess bitter, pungent, and astringent.

lifestyle_guidelines.exercise: Gentle to moderate exercise: walking, swimming, tai chi. Avoid exhausting workouts.
lifestyle_guidelines.sleep: Early to bed (by 10 PM), 7-8 hours. Warm oil foot massage (Abhyanga) before sleep.
daily_routine: Warm Abhyanga (sesame oil self-massage) every morning
Regular meal times — never skip breakfast
Warm bath or shower
Gentle Pranayama (Nadi Shodhana / Anulom Vilom)
Meditation for 10-15 minutes
Avoid excessive travel or stimulation
Stay warm and protected from wind and cold

season_advice.winter: Increase warming foods, oil massage, stay indoors. Use heavier blankets.
season_advice.summer: Moderate activity, sweet and cooling fruits allowed.
season_advice.monsoon: Avoid cold, damp exposure. Prefer warm soups and ginger tea.
season_advice.general: Vata is most aggravated in autumn and early winter — take extra care during Sharad and Hemant ritu.

yoga_recommendations.purpose: Relieves gas and bloating, calms Vata in abdomen
Grounding and calming for nervous system

pacifying_factors.season: Late spring and summer with moderate warmth
pacifying_factors.time: Consistent daily routine with fixed meal and sleep times

extra_dietary: Triphala with warm water at bedtime
Increase ghee and oil in diet
extra_lifestyle: Warm water first thing in morning

extra_dietary: Warm milk with turmeric and ginger
Castor oil with warm milk weekly
extra_lifestyle: Warm sesame oil joint massage daily

Pitta.general_principles: Pitta dosha is governed by fire and water elements. Balance through cooling foods, moderate activity, and avoiding excessive heat, competition, and spicy foods.

favor: Leafy greens and cooling vegetables (cucumber, zucchini, asparagus)
Rose water and aloe vera juice
avoid: Excess sour, salty, and pungent tastes
dietary_guidelines.meal_timing: Regular meals; largest meal at noon when Agni is strongest. Do not skip lunch. Light dinner by 7 PM.
dietary_guidelines.taste_preference: Sweet, bitter, and astringent tastes pacify Pitta. Minimize sour, salty, and pungent.

lifestyle_guidelines.exercise: Moderate exercise in cool hours — morning or evening. Swimming is ideal. Avoid midday sun and competitive sports.
lifestyle_guidelines.sleep: Sleep by 10 PM. Cool bedroom. Sandalwood or rose on temples before sleep.
daily_routine: Coconut oil or sunflower oil Abhyanga (cooling oils)
Avoid midday sun exposure (10 AM - 3 PM)
Sheetali or Sheetkari Pranayama for cooling
Moonlight walks in the evening
Wear light-colored, cotton clothing
Practice compassion meditation (Metta/Karuna)
Cool water wash for eyes (Netra Prakshalana)

season_advice.summer: Critical season — maximize cooling foods, avoid sun. Sandalwood paste, moonlight walks.
season_advice.winter: Pitta is naturally calmer; moderate warming foods are acceptable.
season_advice.monsoon: Agni tends to be low; eat light, avoid heavy and fermented foods.
season_advice.general: Pitta is most aggravated in summer and late spring — take extra care during Grishma ritu.

yoga_recommendations.purpose: Massages liver and digestive organs, releases Pitta heat
Direct cooling breath, reduces body temperature
Opens chest, releases stored emotional heat
Calms the nervous system, cools the head

aggravating_factors.time: 10 AM-2 PM and 10 PM-2 AM (Pitta kala)

preferred_dosage_forms: Churna with cool water or milk

extra_lifestyle: Do not eat spicy food at night

Kapha.general_principles: Kapha dosha is governed by earth and water elements. Balance through light, dry, and warm foods, vigorous exercise, and stimulating activities. Avoid heaviness, excess sleep, and cold damp conditions.

favor: Spices: black pepper, ginger, turmeric, mustard, cayenne
Light grains: barley, millet, buckwheat, corn
Green tea, ginger tea, spiced herbal infusions
avoid: Cold dairy products (ice cream, cold milk, cheese)
Excessive water intake (drink only when thirsty)
dietary_guidelines.meal_timing: Two main meals (skip breakfast or keep very light). Largest meal at noon. No eating after sunset if possible.
dietary_guidelines.taste_preference: Pungent, bitter, and astringent tastes pacify Kapha. Minimize sweet, sour, and salty.

lifestyle_guidelines.exercise: Vigorous daily exercise is essential — running, brisk walking, cycling, aerobics. Exercise in the morning for best effect.
lifestyle_guidelines.sleep: Rise before 6 AM (before Kapha time). Avoid daytime sleeping. 6-7 hours sufficient.
daily_routine: Wake before sunrise (5-6 AM)
Dry powder massage (Udvartana) with herbal powders
Vigorous exercise or brisk walk in morning
Kapalabhati and Bhastrika Pranayama
Avoid daytime napping — stay active
Keep environment warm, dry, and bright
Engage in stimulating mental activities

season_advice.spring: Critical season — Kapha accumulates. Fasting, dry massage, extra exercise recommended.
season_advice.winter: Avoid heavy foods; keep active despite cold weather.
season_advice.monsoon: Kapha aggravated by dampness — use dry, warm, spicy foods.
season_advice.general: Kapha is most aggravated in spring and late winter — take extra care during Vasant and Shishira ritu.

extra_dietary: Honey with warm water on empty stomach
Avoid all sweets and fried foods
extra_lifestyle: Morning jog or brisk walk 45 min

Vata-Pitta.general_principles: Vata-Pitta dual prakriti combines air, space, fire, and water. Balance through moderately warm but not overheating foods. Maintain regularity without rigidity. Manage both anxiety (Vata) and irritability (Pitta).

favor: Ghee and olive oil in moderate amounts
Well-cooked grains: basmati rice, wheat, oats
Mild spices: cumin, coriander, fennel, turmeric
avoid: Extremely spicy or extremely cold foods
Very dry or very oily foods in excess
dietary_guidelines.meal_timing: Three regular meals with warm food. Lunch as the largest. Avoid late dinners.
dietary_guidelines.taste_preference: Favor sweet taste (pacifies both). Use moderate sour/salty (controls Vata without over-stimulating Pitta).

lifestyle_guidelines.exercise: Moderate exercise: yoga, swimming, walking. Avoid extremes — not too vigorous (Pitta), not too gentle (Vata stagnation).
lifestyle_guidelines.sleep: 7-8 hours. Consistent bedtime by 10 PM. Warm (not hot) oil massage before sleep.
daily_routine: Moderate-temperature Abhyanga with coconut-sesame oil blend
Regular schedule for meals, sleep, and work
Nadi Shodhana Pranayama — balances both doshas
Nature walks in moderate weather
Creative expression (art, music, writing)
Avoid overcommitting and burnout
10-15 min mindfulness meditation

season_advice.summer: Focus on Pitta management: cooling foods, avoid midday sun.
season_advice.winter: Focus on Vata management: warming foods, oil massage, stay warm.
season_advice.monsoon: Balance both: warm, light, fresh foods. Avoid heavy and cold.
season_advice.general: Adjust emphasis seasonally — prioritize the dosha most aggravated by current season.

yoga_recommendations.purpose: Stabilizing and opening — balances both doshas
Harmonizes nervous system, balances Vata and Pitta
Calming forward bend, cools Pitta while grounding Vata
Deep rest and nervous system reset

aggravating_factors.season: Autumn (Vata) and summer (Pitta) are both challenging

Pitta-Kapha.general_principles: Pitta-Kapha dual prakriti has strong metabolism (Pitta) with sturdy build (Kapha). Balance through moderately light and cooling diet. Needs regular vigorous exercise but must avoid overheating. Manage both heat and heaviness.

favor: Light, warm foods with bitter and astringent tastes
Moderate spices: turmeric, coriander, fennel, cumin
Fresh fruits: apples, pears, berries, pomegranate
avoid: Red meat and heavy dairy (cheese, cream)
Excess sweets and cold desserts (aggravates Kapha)
dietary_guidelines.meal_timing: Two to three moderate meals. Lunch is primary. Light dinner. Avoid snacking between meals.
dietary_guidelines.taste_preference: Bitter and astringent pacify both. Use moderate pungent (stimulates Kapha without over-heating Pitta).

lifestyle_guidelines.exercise: Regular vigorous exercise in cool hours — morning runs, swimming, cycling. Essential for Kapha but schedule in cool times for Pitta.
lifestyle_guidelines.sleep: 6-7 hours. Rise early (before 6 AM). Avoid daytime sleeping.
daily_routine: Early morning exercise routine (5:30-7:00 AM)
Light dry massage on Kapha days, cooling oil on Pitta days
Alternate between stimulating and cooling pranayama
Avoid afternoon lethargy — stay active
Evening walks near water bodies
Keep workspace organized and decluttered
Competitive sports in moderation (avoid excess intensity)

season_advice.summer: Focus on Pitta: cooling foods, swimming, avoid midday sun.
season_advice.spring: Focus on Kapha: fasting, vigorous exercise, dry massage.
season_advice.winter: Both doshas benefit from moderate warmth and activity.
season_advice.general: Spring and summer require most attention — Kapha in spring, Pitta in summer.

yoga_recommendations.purpose: Builds heat to counter Kapha without excess Pitta
Opens throat and chest, balances thyroid

diet: Spicy food combined with rich meals

Vata-Kapha.general_principles: Vata-Kapha dual prakriti alternates between lightness/dryness (Vata) and heaviness/dampness (Kapha). Balance through warm, light, and mildly spiced foods. Needs both grounding (for Vata) and stimulation (for Kapha). Regularity is key.

favor: Warm soups and stews with ginger and black pepper
Light grains: basmati rice (small portions), millet, quinoa
Well-cooked mung dal and split legumes
Warm spices: ginger, cinnamon, black pepper, turmeric, cumin
avoid: Cold dairy products and ice cream
Heavy grains in excess (wheat, sticky rice)
dietary_guidelines.meal_timing: Three light meals with consistent timing. Warm breakfast. Moderate lunch. Light, early dinner.
dietary_guidelines.taste_preference: Pungent and warming tastes pacify both. Sweet in moderation (grounding without heaviness).

lifestyle_guidelines.exercise: Regular moderate-to-vigorous exercise — brisk walking, cycling, dance. Consistency matters more than intensity.
lifestyle_guidelines.sleep: 7 hours. Fixed sleep-wake schedule. Avoid daytime napping. Warm bed, comfortable room.
daily_routine: Warm sesame oil Abhyanga (counters Vata) followed by dry toweling (counters Kapha)
Morning exercise routine — consistent daily
Bhastrika and Nadi Shodhana Pranayama alternated
Stay warm but avoid excess humidity
Engage in mentally stimulating activities
Social connections to counter isolation tendencies
Warm herbal teas throughout the day

season_advice.winter: Most challenging — both doshas aggravated. Extra warmth, exercise, spiced foods.
season_advice.monsoon: Dampness aggravates Kapha; cold wind aggravates Vata. Stay warm and dry.
season_advice.summer: Best season — warmth helps both doshas. Stay moderately active.
season_advice.general: Winter and monsoon are most challenging — maintain warmth, regularity, and spiced foods.

yoga_recommendations.purpose: Builds warmth and strength, counters both doshas
Warming breath, clears Kapha while grounding Vata

pacifying_factors.season: Warm, dry summer and early autumn
pacifying_factors.time: Active morning routine with warming practices

preferred_dosage_forms: Churna with warm water and honey

Sama.general_principles: Sama (Tridosha balanced) prakriti is the ideal constitution. Maintain balance through seasonal adjustment, moderate habits in all areas, and varied diet. The goal is prevention and rhythm rather than correction.

favor: Seasonal and locally grown fresh foods
Balanced use of all six tastes (Shadrasa)
Freshly cooked meals with moderate spicing
Whole grains, fresh vegetables, and seasonal fruits
Ghee, milk, and buttermilk in moderation
Nuts and seeds in small amounts
avoid: Extreme diets or fasting without reason
Food combinations warned against in Ayurveda (Viruddha Ahara)
dietary_guidelines.meal_timing: Three balanced meals at consistent times. Eat mindfully. Follow seasonal meal patterns.
dietary_guidelines.taste_preference: Use all six tastes in balance. Adjust emphasis based on current season and any temporary imbalance.

lifestyle_guidelines.exercise: Varied exercise regimen — yoga, walking, swimming, sports. Adjust intensity with seasons.
lifestyle_guidelines.sleep: 7-8 hours. Natural sleep-wake cycle aligned with sunrise/sunset.
daily_routine: Dinacharya (traditional daily routine) as described in classical texts
Oil massage 2-3 times per week (alternate oils by season)
Pranayama practice — alternate between warming and cooling based on season
Regular meditation practice
Seasonal cleansing (Ritucharya transitions)
Maintain work-life balance
Engage in creative and intellectual pursuits

season_advice.spring: Lighter foods, more exercise, Kapalabhati. Reduce sweet and oily.
season_advice.summer: Cooling foods, moderate activity, Sheetali Pranayama. Increase sweet and bitter.
season_advice.monsoon: Light, warm, easy-to-digest foods. Ginger tea. Avoid heavy meals.
season_advice.autumn: Warming and grounding. Moderate sweet and sour. Oil massage.
season_advice.winter: Nourishing, warming foods. Sesame oil Abhyanga. More sleep allowed.
season_advice.general: The key for Sama prakriti is Ritucharya — adjusting lifestyle with each season to maintain balance.

yoga_recommendations.purpose: Rotate between Nadi Shodhana, Sheetali, Bhastrika by season

aggravating_factors.season: Season transitions if not managed through Ritucharya
aggravating_factors.time: Irregular lifestyle, jet lag, shift work

pacifying_factors.time: Regular natural rhythm aligned with sun cycle
